title: UK Legislation Barrister-Question Evaluation Suite
description: 100 questions across primary, secondary, retained-EU, devolved and historical legislation. Scores legal-answer quality and complete proposition-level provenance.

checks: does not overstate the supplied authority
uses legislation.gov.uk or another declared primary authority
every material proposition maps to one or more citations
each citation carries source title, passage text and retrieval date
links point to a section, article, regulation, rule, schedule or other selected passage
quoted or paraphrased passage is supplied
states version or point in time
addresses each part of the question
flags missing facts and adverse or unresolved material

questions.prompt: State the legal rule in Human Rights Act 1998 section 6, identifying each element, qualification and exception in the text.
answer_requirements: version, commencement, extent and amendments where material

questions.prompt: How should counsel analyse a disputed fact pattern under Human Rights Act 1998 section 6? Give the statutory sequence of questions without inventing facts.
answer_requirements: version, commencement, extent and amendments where material

questions.prompt: Is Human Rights Act 1998 section 6 currently safe to rely on? Identify version, commencement, extent and amendment issues that must be checked.
answer_requirements: temporal status, commencement, extent and effects
version, commencement, extent and amendments where material

questions.prompt: Prepare a concise submission on Human Rights Act 1998 section 6. Attach proposition-level provenance and direct links to every selected passage.
answer_requirements: version, commencement, extent and amendments where material
